message: Jake
Yuriko
Boss, Boss, look at this
Please look at this!
Huh, what is it?
As I was told to watch TV, the news was just
starting to air.
What on earth is this?
It was a tragic accident. Some weekly magazines reported that these two had already broken up,
but it seems that wasn't actually the case. Now it seems we are connected to the scene. Weber?
Yes, this is the scene. Look at the tire tracks, they lead straight off the cliff.
From what I can see of the tire marks, there are no signs of braking.
This supposedly seems to be because the brakes of the car that Guy rented were broken. It's said this wasn't an accident.
However, some questions remain. No matter how muddy the road was, it’s a gentle curve.
Furthermore, where I’m standing, you have to deliberately turn the steering wheel against the curve
and break through the roadblock to get in.
From this, whispers of a suicide theory are being floated.
That’s all from the scene full of mysteries.
Thank you, Weber. I see, so the brakes were broken.
However, what concerns me is whether it was an accident or suicide. If it was suicide, it could be viewed as an atonement for killing Cindy.
I can't help but spiral into such thoughts.
Of course, this is just my personal opinion. It's merely speculation.
However, listening to the reporter, they went into a location where they had to deliberately turn the steering wheel to get in and then had an accident.
Is it just my curiosity that makes me doubt whether this can be considered merely an accident?
Still, it was an amazing place. Even the great Guy must have struggled after falling from such a steep cliff.
I pray for the souls of Guy and Kristy.
Welcome back, Boss. Just kidding.
Thank you for your hard work.
See.
Speak.
Listen.
Think.
Move.
System.
Surroundings.
About Guy.
About Kristy.
About Meryl.
About Jennifer.
About Director Fabar.
About the serial murder case.
About Guy.
About Kristy.
About Meryl.
About Jennifer.
About Director Fabar.
About the serial murder case.
To another place.
Save.
Exit the game.
Bare minimum.
Other than my desk, phone, and reception set,
there's almost nothing.
It's a narrow office with hardly anything in it, but it's better than a police holding cell. At least I have a door to leave the room anytime I want.
After all, my familiar office feels so comforting.
I also realized there are much more uncomfortable rooms than this one.
Nothing has changed since before I was arrested. I haven't lost any weight, and my complexion is as healthy as ever.
Even though my employer is detained and I'm out of work, I've been managing the office all by myself. My face is somewhat.
No different.
I kind of have a happy expression. Maybe it’s better having this unreliable boss around than not having him at all?
Ah.
The joy of having the boss here.
Heh.
What a creepy thing to say.
I mean, at one point, I wondered what was going to happen. Was I really going to have to take over the office?
Right?
Oh my.
Saying that while people are savoring their misfortune.
I really don’t like that.
No.
You came to pick me up, didn’t you? Oh, boss!
But I'm glad. That the boss could come back like this.
If he hadn’t come back, I would have been framed as a murderer, you know?
Ah, no.
That’s not going to happen, right, boss?
What did you do while you were detained?
What did I do? I was just organizing everything up to now and thinking about the murderer involved in the nearby cases.
Huh.
So, did you have any leads on the murderer?
Yeah.
Who did you think was suspicious, boss?
Boss.
Please don’t get into any trouble from now on.
Guy is traveling right now, isn’t he? When will he come back?
Do you suspect Guy too, boss?
Even though I was under house arrest, I don’t know how much of it was true, and the recent victims have mostly been women connected to Guy.
Ah.
It really is, isn’t it? It makes you think about jealous Kristy or perhaps even the person themselves.
You just can't help but wonder.
But, no way Guy is.
Traveling with Guy, right? They must be having an amazing time now.
Ah, so jealous.
The idea that Kristy is the culprit.
When you think about it, it does kind of fit, I guess.
It’s hard to suspect a friend, but looking back on her past, it’s hard not to think that way.
Yeah.
But.
But, Kristy doesn’t have the ability to cut someone’s body.
Speaking of which, I wonder how Meryl is doing?
Well,
you’re not saying she got arrested over that incident, are you?
If they say Meryl's a murderer, I feel like I could actually believe it.
But there's no way she’d kill her own daughter.
Meryl probably didn’t even know Cindy was her daughter.
But still, her motive isn’t clear.
Considering a gruesome murder, that's true. But still,
Ugh, living like this, doubting people, isn’t good.
No way Meryl would.
That can’t be, right?
Jennifer called me and when I told her about Boss getting taken in, she talked to the higher-ups in the police.
Is that so?
Yeah. Celebrity status really pays off in situations like this.
The Boss has to thank Jennifer.
How's the director doing? I still want to run for the wife candidate.
That's just for the money.
Oh ho ho ho ho.
I think you're the richest among my acquaintances for now. Oh, maybe the Boss is actually the richest?
The director is probably worried about you too.
I don't know about that. First of all, doesn’t the director not know that I was detained?
Ah, that's what I heard. It seems it was relayed through Jennifer.
It's great that someone is worried about you.
You can't make your grandfather worry, you know.
I wonder if the police are convinced Boss isn't the culprit.
Boss, who do they think is the perpetrator?
They think it could be Kristy, or she and Guy could be accomplices.
Boss!
Aren't you being cold to your friends?
I don’t want to believe they could be murderers either. But considering the motive and circumstances, it has to be them.
No way.
Certainly, in terms of motive, Kristy seems suspicious.
But committing murder and dismembering a body is impossible.
Where did Guy take Kristy? It seems even the people involved don’t know.
I get it that they want to take a break after everything that happened in New York, but it's a hassle for me.
They seem to not understand that their suspicions aren’t completely cleared yet. It’s quite troubling.
I wonder if they’re just acting normally on their trip. I hope the sudden change of environment doesn’t cause any illness to resurface.
Kristy still can’t be ruled out from the suspect list. How long will I have to keep doubting her?
If what Günter said is correct, it feels like the real culprit can only be Kristy.
I wonder if they still think Guy is the murderer of Cindy. I need to clear Meryl's misunderstanding before it’s too late.
Watching the scene at the party, it feels like the same thing might repeat over and over again. I can't take my eyes off Meryl.
I have to persuade him not to do anything reckless, no matter what. It was a good thing it was just an attempt, but if he really stabbed someone, it would be a huge problem.
I wonder what whim made them save me from the police?
I need to thank Jennifer.
I hope it isn't a bother if I visit the hotel.
Does Jennifer really like me? Otherwise, she wouldn't go out of her way to help me.
I wonder if the director believes I'm the culprit? The reason the shoot got canceled was due to a cast member's death, and was it really ruined because of me?
Aren't they going back to Boston yet? Don't they want to end their terrible stay in New York soon?
They might know where Guy and the others are.
The police still seem unable to narrow down the suspect. Making a detective like me a suspect is proof that solving this case is still far off.
The one who looks most suspicious right now is.
It really is Guy and Kristy, huh? I can't be the only one thinking that.
We need to catch the culprit before the next victim appears.
※※※ It's a bug. ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
